reviews.review_text:
I just finished God of War Ragnarök on Give Me God of War and I ended up getting 100% too, which made it feel more satisfying.

The Berserker Gravestones was my favorite side content in the game and it felt personal. One of those fights took me hours, the Berserker Sisters. I got destroyed over and over trying to beat them

After beating the sisters, the rest of the combat started feeling way easier because that fight basically forces you to learn every mechanic properly. I even managed to do a no-hit vs Gná 
which I never thought I’d be able to do when I first started the game. The combat is just so damn good... Every weapon feels useful even tho I wasn't a fan of the spear, parrying feels super satisfyin, and I loved playing around with the gear and trying all sorts of builds!

I know, I know... Some people don't like how Kratos has gone soft but come on...
Kratos is the best. Growing up with the older games, seeing how much he’s changed in this game hit surprisingly hard at times, emotionally. They handled his character so well. There were a few moments where I just sat there after a cutscene thinking "damn"

Very few games leave me with that empty feeling, but Ragnarök definitely did. Easily one of those must play single player games if you love a great story + gameplay

with all that said F Sony for cutting out all future PC ports :)

wow just wow. 
its just god of war 2017 but 10x better. was waiting for the summer sale to get this but I just couldn't. 

been non stop a good time. 12 hrs deep and I'm loving every second of it.
story is amazing and that gameplay??? somehow 10x better than 2017
10/10 One of the best games I've ever played, and some of the most mature and nuanced storytelling in any medium.
oh my god this game actually made me cry it gave me so much emotion despite me barely being able to feel them which is weird because i have apathy but its so good!
Very tightly written and compelling story.
Gameplay wise, Its great.  As a continuing story about a father coming to terms with a teenage son as well as his own past, its excellent.  As an exploration of Nordic Mythology... Its awful.

PEAK
takes GOW 4 and fixes almost every flaw but adds some new ones
combat is peak and Valhalla is a great addition but I liked GOW 4's story a little better


Also Atreus missions are dookie
fantastic game, fantastic story of redemption for kratos

A unique game about fathers, sons, complex family relations, defying fate and writing your own story. Definitely a fun experience that may have some bumps along the road. Let's start with the good and make our way to the less so.

-Graphics? Put real world to shame.

-World design? Multiple beautiful open worlds with unique environments, interesting puzzles and a lot of new enemies.

-Gameplay? Very fluid combat, a ton of moves and multiple weapons with their own special attacks.

-RPG Elements? Definitely fun and interesting to put things together and make your own playstyle.

-Dialogue? Very well written, often sounds as real as it gets, and mirrors each character's personality, though sometimes it's mirrored too well to the point that it ranges from epic to cringe.

-Character development? Fitting and fulfilling.

-Cutscenes, motion capture and voice acting? All excellent. The actors' performances are all phenomenal. Some scenes filled me with joy, and other scenes drained it all away. Some scenes brought me to tears, others dropped my jaw in awe.

-Story? Definitely great, tight and emotional. Story pacing on the other hand is not okay. Some scenes drag far too long, and some arcs or quests definitely needed more fleshing out. There's this one famous gameplay sequence that brings the story to a halt for hours. Definitely needs fixing.

-Music? I mean... it's there and a maybe a few pieces are memorable.

-Replay-ability? Not so much. Despite the gripping story and the vast fun open world, the game's pacing problems, especially the Atreus parts, in addition to the fact that you'd already know all the story's plot twists, kill the game's replay-ability. Personally I just finished the base game's achievements and everything it has to offer and now I'm moving on to Valhalla.

Overall the game is a must play at least once. If Santa Monica were to fix the pacing problems with the Atreus segments, and maybe flesh out Ragnarok more into maybe 4-5 more main quests, then this game would be a 10. As it stands.... I think it's a 7.
i hope sony keeps bringing games on steam. gow series is one of the best adventure/action games out there
really fun but sometimes lagging but its probably cause i have graphics on the highest settings
La Historia no es mejor que el GoW 2018, pero el gameplay/combate es mejor. Graficamente tmb le hace 6-0 al 2018
i love this game it was very fun i loved the boss fights and some of them were tuff
Good game if you liked 2018, but the story wasn't as tight and some of the characters were a bit annoying toward the end.  Overall the story felt too rushed
Great game love the story so far. combat is pretty good but maybe a little hard on pc or maybe i just suck. i dont like that the enemies can just chonk ur health so fast.
great game not as good as 2018 but that thing was a mastgerpiece. a solid 8/10 game and valhalla is amazing

God of Hope.

Que perro juegazo la verdad.

Nada como aprender acerca del perdon propio mientras masacras a la mitad de la población.

After 15 years without playing video games, I decided to give God of War Ragnarok a try, and honestly, the game far exceeded my expectations.
What has resonated with me most, as a father, is the conversation between Kratos and his son. There are moments that make you rethink how you approach your children, especially as they enter their teenage years. The way that the relationship develops is profound, realistic, and very moving. I like it.
Beyond the spectacular production and fluid combat, the game has an emotional heart that I didn't expect to find. So far, I'm enjoying it immensely, and I think it's a sensational and highly entertaining title. Highly recommended vamooossssssss!!!.
Great Story, good graphics, got DLC for free
there is really no words for me to say this game is a complete masterpiece, kratos is one of the best video game characters ever mannn 🥹

Excellent game! I really, really love it.
The combat system is sharp, the story is astounding, and the graphics are gorgeous.

Gameplay is an upgrade from 2018, story was slightly dissapointing, but overall still very good. It suffers from the same problem many sequels do where they try to expand the story and it suffers for it, however, the story is still very good and it has an amazing sense of scale and finality to it.
God of war is a must. Some graphic and sound glitches classic from PS ports, but it doesnt matter, insane game.
A really well made game. Storyline, gameplay, and picture perfect graphics. We want to see Kratos back someday.

Ya, it's good and still worth a thumbs up, but nothing as good as the first. The touching moments between father and son are few and there is now some really cringe stories and dialogs. The story is more fetch quests and not as immersive

Insanely fun and challenging game... Love the story arch and characters. Quests are, maybe a bit linear at times, but still super entertaining, and the game is absolutely amazeballs to look at. The only problem I've had at all is with the difficulty settings... On the higher difficulty settings, it can be 'Souls' level challenging, but if you drop it down the the "balanced" setting to catch your breath, you are suddenly so OP that you are basically invincible and find yourself getting bored as. All in all, this sweet baby needs to be played, full-stop. Layniac out

I’ve been playing these games since the originals on the PS2, and I honestly couldn't have asked for a better ending to Kratos's story. Ragnarök improves on the 2018 game in pretty much every way, the combat is faster and feels way more brutal, and exploring the different realms is just breathtaking. But what really hooked me was the writing. Watching Kratos go from a mindless, raging monster to a tired dad just trying to guide his son has been an incredible journey, and the way they wrapped it all up left me feeling fulfilled. Seeing him finally find a path toward peace and healing, rather than just more endless vengeance, felt so incredibly earned. That final sequence left me completely floored and a bit emotional, to be honest. Santa Monica Studio did right by this character and his history, and as a long-time fan, it was everything I wanted it to be and more.

Absolute masterpiece. Better in every way over the PS4 release; never crashed or had issues once in over 100 hrs.
Fantastic gameplay, lengthy story, dozens of side quests and a heavily replayable DLC. 

Genuinely, you can't ask for more. 10/10

I like the game, but I swear I haven't played a game where the easiest difficulty is still one of the hardest things I've ever played. "Give Me Story" feels more like 10% story and 90% extremely hard fighting. I know the game is based on combat, but the first game was at least playable without dying 50+ times for each battle.
Minus the Atreus sections, great game. Except when it seems to randomly have problems with terrible frame rate drops with normal cpu and gpu temps.

if you played the first game this is kinda the same, in a good way - story, gameplay, exploration, long, lots of fun. had only 1 bug during the gameplay which is well documented in various forums, other then that the game works perfectly

Incredible game. Seriously incredible game, a true AAA experience compared to other "AAA Developers".

Combat, story, graphics, performance, audio, characters, mechanics, literally every single thing in this game feels polished and perfect. This game is a masterpiece.

Ragnarok takes everything that made God of War great and elevates it to another level with a bigger story, better combat and unforgettable moments throughout. A very strong sequel that delivers all the way through. Definitely worth experiencing.
nice graphics,story,enviroment and characters
Excellent game, and a wonderful dad lesson simulator.

This games great, it adds onto the combat from the last game and its greatly optimized especially for low end computers like mine. I get a consistent 60-90 fps the whole time I'm playing and its never crashed on me. Def recommend playing God of War 2018 first before playing this one.
Amazing game. The story is rich, the character development is deep, and the combat is challenging and fun. 120 hours of playtime. 100% completion. 100% recommendation.

This was peak. Finally getting to play it, having enjoyed the hell out of the first game, was so fulfilling. The story was so amazing, I loved how they introduced new elements and created a completely unique story that adds so much richness to the original narrative. I really loved the ending, very beautiful, and it wraps up well, but it hints at possibly more. I loved the themes surrounding this game a bit more than the first, felt more powerful, fulfilling and hopeful, with great amounts of character growth. 

The gameplay of course was great, and visually the game is stunning. I feel as though great graphics and visuals are synonymous with most, if not all Playstation exclusives. There were so many moments that just had me in awe, and had me question if this was a video game. I also loved the fact that they added the ability to play as Atreus. It adds so much more depth to his character and allows you to really connect with his character.

Finally the performances, my god. Christopher Judge and Sunny Suljic, I have no words. I will be honest, there were definitely a lot of moments in the game that hit emotionally hard. With that being said, I believe this to be a perfect game in my eyes, and a perfect sequel. I don't mind anything that involves in-game issues/mechanics, as it doesn't bother me. I'm more concerned about the story, and often the visuals. And with this game, the story and visuals make it that perfect game.
game is very interesting and is very beautiful well spent money in my opinion

Easily one of the greatest games I have ever played. Excellent story, combat, environment, mechanics, and overall experience. Every moment keeps you entertained whether it be through action or dialogue resulting in a compelling story built around exciting gameplay that keeps you coming back. 

10/10, make sure to play GoW 2018 too.
Great sound design, visuals, combat, and solid story-telling. Valhalla is a great addition for those who want to engage in the combat system without replaying entire story,
It's a fun game. The mechanics are smooth, the story is great, the characters are awesome. The world is built out really well. 10/10 one of the best games I've played.

Likely one of the best game of all time. There is no any better game includes semi open word, deep cinematic storyline and RPG. Masterpiece.

Worth to mention, producers made an excellent work to separate second part of the game and make it different.

God of War Ragnarök is one of the best story games i've  played. im not with it but i find it very fun and interesting since i can do so much more things in this game than in the first one. I personally like this way better than the first god of war and i definitely reccomend.
An amazing ending to the Norse storyline with great combat, weapons and visuals. And the free Valhalla DLC is just as amazing and fun! 10/10!
This is so far peak PS games I've played for PC. Honestly, would recommend to anyone who likes action or storyline games.

Well Worth the purchase! Stunning game from visuals to audio to gameplay and the continuation after completing should be the industry standard.The Boss fights the hooks this game gets into you. A Personal 10/10. Thank you Playstation for bringing this to PC and the trophies sync is so cool.Platinumed in 94Hrs.
Berserkers were brutal that KING..UGH.

Very good game i played the whole trilogy and this one is by far one of my top 3 of the trilogy it runs super smooth the combat is great it is just an overall good game highly recommend playing same with GOW 2018
Incredible history, amazing graphics, satisfying gameplay mechanics and boss fights

God of War Ragnarök ⭐ Recommended
33.4 hours played
Kratos and I were having a beautiful father-son journey across the Nine Realms. We slayed gods. We cried. We yelled “BOY” at things. Then Windrose came into my life and I haven’t opened this game in months. If you’re reading this Kratos, I’m sorry. The sea called. Atreus, tell your dad I love him.
12/48 achievements is just enough to look like I tried.
Small critique: Too many puzzles not enough fighting.. But the game is still good.

God of War Ragnarök was one of the most incredible gaming experiences I’ve ever had. Every part of it felt polished and unforgettable, from the breathtaking world design and intense combat to the emotional story and character development that kept me invested the entire way through. The relationship between Kratos and Atreus was written so well that every moment felt meaningful, and the scale of the world somehow felt massive while still personal. The visuals were stunning, the soundtrack was powerful, and every boss fight felt epic and rewarding. It’s one of those rare games where every aspect comes together perfectly and reminds you why video games can be such an amazing experience.

[h1]Dadnarök is Here[/h1]

The [b]Dad of Boy[/b] returns as he prepares [b]Boy[/b] for [b]Dadröck[/b] and the trials that follow.

Honestly, an amazing sequel with only a few nitpicks. On the other hand, this story has one of the most well executed protrayals of [b]healthy masculinity[/b], what it means to be a Father, and the lengths one would go for their child.
good game, but wrath of the frost ancient is nerfed and that sucks... i cannot wait for GOW 6
Although it’s very similar to the first game, it features plenty of side quests and an engaging overarching story. A true father-son story...
Great game like the one before it.  I was curious how they'd keep it similar yet fresh and they nailed it.  Having a lot of fun and expect to play out the side elements to stretch it out.

It didn't have quite the same level of excitement for me that the previous title did (being someone that grew up playing all the originals), but I still enjoyed it. The gameplay itself was moderately enjoyable, but I enjoyed this much more for the cinematic nature of the game. Solid story with great acting. I even teared up a few times!
Run pretty good on my rig load times be long for areas but we chillin the dougie and the jets
Great game, lots of fun and challenging levels are optional for those that want to enjoy a story. It is a long game for the casual gamer so even for me it is still enjoyable.
The best game of all time kratos the ghost of sparta man enjoyed every minutes of this master piece platinum with 68 hours

More like God of Puzzles. Who put fighting in my puzzle game? This was my 2nd GoW game with 2018 being my first. I loved that game and was excited to get into this one, but what the hell did they cook up in the dev room because this is ASS. I loved the relationship between Kratos, Atreus, and Mimir. Then very quickly Atreus is doing a solo mission, and you know what. I didn't even mind it, I thought it was fun. Unfortunately, it quickly escalated, the entire game feels like its without the original gang. The Atreus missions got bad quickly it took everything to keep playing these god awful boring missions. But hey at least the game is pretty ig.

Not as good as the first installment.  
Politics over Story consistancy
Gimmiks over Story flow
Padding over substance
overall a sad disappointment in the great story of Kratos.

That means games like Spider-Man, God of War, Ghost of Tsushima, Horizon, etc if they get sequels they will not be coming to PC so it is a waste to even buy them. Seems like a great game but it crashes and freezes constantly. First, I'm having d3d crashes so I turn off frame generation and it's working fine for a couple hours. Now it freezes within a couple mins from what I read is most likely caused by needing  16bit, 44100 Hz (CD Quality) audio specifically or it will freeze. I don't have headphones or speakers where I can change it but at this point ya"ll got my broke A$$ basically paying to debug something that is not in a problem in any of the dozens of games I"ve played.

SUMMARY: SONY WENT CHEAP ON THE PORTING FOR THIS PARTICULAR GAME TO MAXAMIZE PROFIT.  WHAT IS IT WITH CORPORATIONS LATELY AND MOVING LIKE TRUST FUND BABIES? RANT OVER. PEACE.\

Update: Using DLSS Swapper and updating the DLSS and frame generation version to the latest version seems to have fixed it. Even though it works fine now and the game itself is great, I still can't recommend a game that doesn't work unless I use 3rd party software to modify the game files.
Permanently crashing, freezing or disconnecting devices. Almost two years since release and game is still not patched. Nice artwork, voice acting and setting but abysmal technical implementation.

Doesn't feel like old times, Mid game id rather play the originals

I really wanted to like this game. I really did. I gave it a full 15 hours, convinced that eventually it would hook me. It never did. If anything, it felt like watching the dying gasps of Grandpa Kratos while longtime fans of the original series gather around his deathbed to pay their final respects.

After forcing myself through yet another mind-numbingly dull puzzle, I finally shut the game off and went to play Hades II instead.

TLDR: My biggest takeaways are the endless braindead puzzles, uninspired characters who constantly dump lore and trivia on you, and the surprisingly sparse amount of combat, even though the combat itself is great. This no longer feels like God of War. It feels more like God of Talk: The Endless Puzzle.

Like many people, I grew up loving the original God of War games. As a teen, I was hooked. They were brutal, fast-paced, combat-focused, and exceedingly edgy. When the series was rebooted in 2018, it undeniably moved away from many of those elements, but I still enjoyed it. While the game focused more on world-building, exploration, and mystery, it still gave us glimpses of the old Kratos and enough nostalgia to keep longtime fans invested.

With God of War: Ragnarok, however, I genuinely don't know what happened to either the writing or gameplay teams. Gone is the sense of mystery and discovery that made the 2018 game compelling. Instead, you're constantly bombarded with exposition. Want to hear a hundred facts about the history of the Light and Dark Elves that have little impact on your experience, story progression, or anticipation for future encounters? Don't worry, Atreus and Mimir have you covered. They'll happily talk your ears off while you're slogging through another series of pointless environmental puzzles.

And oh my god, the puzzles. Those puzzles are, without doubt, some of the least engaging I've ever seen in a game.

Move a mirror. Align an object. Throw your axe at a specific angle. Repeat. Sometimes you'll unlock a chest filled with loot that rarely feels meaningful. Other times you'll simply open another path toward the next puzzle. All the while, Atreus and Mimir blabber their endless stream of trivia about some ancient conflict, forgotten king, or the favorite slippers of the Light Elf Queen. And after they are done lore dumping, if you still haven’t solved the puzzle by some miracle or simply because you fell asleep out of pure boredom, the game panics and immediately splashes a bucketload of yellow paint by having Atreus or Mimir screech ‘Have you tried moving X piece to do Y?’

Fifteen hours in, there was barely any meaningful buildup toward future bosses or major antagonists. I never found myself looking forward to the next big bad because the game never gave me a reason to care. The bosses aren't set up properly as intimidating, mysterious, or particularly memorable. They're simply obstacles standing on the railroad tracks of the story.

The only antagonist I was looking forward to encountering ever again was Thor, however, he hasn't made any appearance past the intro sequence for the rest of my time with the game. Odin felt more like a scheming old man rather than a wise and powerful being, and did not feel threatening or impactful in any way for me to care about him. Despite his ravens, or, as explained by the game, his eyes and ears across the nine realms being present in every nook and corner of each region as the stereotypical 'kill X amount' collectibles, Odin did not make an appearance either. Which was to my great surprise, as it would make sense for him to meddle with Kratos' quest to find Tyr and beyond, given the unamicable disagreement at the start of the game, and to further build him up as an antagonist in the story.

To be fair, the original God of War games weren't exactly great at the set up of in-between bosses either. But the difference is that those games didn't rely on their storytelling nearly as much as this one. They were driven by a satisfying gameplay loop and brutally entertaining cutscenes. Ragnarok, on the other hand, clearly wants the story to be the main attraction. It absolutely is, because combat feels surprisingly rare. Unfortunately, the story never managed to hook me enough to justify that shift in focus.

The storytelling itself rarely felt engaging. Cutscenes dragged on without offering much payoff, and much of the series' original brutality and intensity has been replaced with lengthy conversations about feelings, relationships, and world lore. None of that is inherently bad, but the characters delivering it often feel bland and uninspired. Few have particularly interesting personalities, dynamics, or character traits, and much of their dialogue quickly fades into background noise. A lot of it simply feels convoluted, unfocused, and uninspired.

Ironically, the combat is fantastic. It is also the thing the game seems least interested in emphasizing. Whenever Ragnarok finally lets you fight, it's a blast. Kratos feels powerful and responsive, with a satisfying moveset that rewards mastery. Even Atreus is surprisingly enjoyable to play. Had the game leaned more heavily into its combat and trimmed down the endless procession of puzzles, exposition, and cutscenes, it would have been a much better experience for me. Instead, combat feels like the reward you earn for enduring hours of puzzle-solving and lore dumping.

I understand that my opinion is very much in the minority. Plenty of people love this game, and I can respect that. But as someone who enjoys both great stories and great action games, God of War: Ragnarok didn't hook me on either of those aspects. It feels like a game that wanted to become a deeper, more mature narrative experience while still retaining its action game roots. In the process, it missed the mark on both.

For me, it wasn't an engaging story, nor was it an engaging video game, and I regret the time and money spent on it.

Im just curious how many people have purchased this game for pc with a decent pc and still takes ages to play the game because of the compiling shaders and the graphics are 50x more annoying to adjust just to play the game smoothly cuz im very upset I can't play this game how I want to
The controls in the early game are uncomfortable, making it hard to get in the zone and enjoy the game, despite interesting graphics and an interesting premise.
love it when i pay 70 bucks for a game only for it to not want to use any other controller besides a playstation controller

Yet again, like my other review on God of War 2018, I've replayed God of War Ragnarök on console so many times that I lost count. Once I got a PC, I had to buy God of War Ragnarök on Steam too. I also don't know why this game gets so much hate. Other than that, it's an amazing game, in my opinion, and it does almost everything that God of War 2018 did, but better except for the story.

Amazing graphics, amazing combat mechanics, great story. Cons: difficulty is not even close to balanced in terms of reward-to-effort (Souls vet here, already beat gna and the berserkr king, both twice in gmgow difficulty), not worth choosing the hardest difficulty beside bragging rights, no extra rewards. Absolutely worth buying imo tho.

Came to finally review this game after PlayStation's State of Play today...

This is an awesome game, and highly recommend; but so long, Kratos. I'm going to miss you. I will not be playing the follow up to this game, as I won't be able to play as, you know, the God of War.

RIP God of War
Liked It as much as GoW 2018, combat is better, world is more immersive, story couldn´t beat the last one but that was an imposible task anyways.

Sony keeps proving why Single-Player, narrative-driven games are some of the most memorable entertainment experiences on the planet.

I only have two gripes with GoW:R. One is how quickly companions solve puzzles for you even if you put the setting for puzzle tips on minimum. The second one is how much stuff you run into that can only be accessible much later - something borrowed from open world titles that should have stayed there.

Still, well worth the money and many hours I will not forget about. The writing is something that will stick with me forever.

as someone who loved the original trilogy and the spin-offs and somehow liked the new approach of the prequel - i just can't get into this one. i really want to but simply can't. the sound, quality of graphics and design are topnotch as always as is kratos as a character and his depth. HOWEVER:

the story in a whole feels flat, without a really "oomph" up to now. the prequel handled that better. it's just not catching enough to want me to play more. atreus might be in his puberty but is rather annoying as a companion. the riddles are boring. the world feels void. what really frustrates me, though, is how the gameplay feels bland, lackluster and repetitive. the "new" gameplay, like in the prequel, is still massive inferior to its original. there is literally no thrill. they reduced finishing moves and throws.

dunno. i could literally start any old gow game and play it through without a break because they are so captivating in comparison.

Wish it wasn't so linear. Loved playing as Kratos, not a fan of the Atreus moveset or plot line. This studio is losing the plot with this game. We buy the game, and play the game... for Kratos. TOO MANY CUTSCENES. We want to run around with huge axes and tear stuff apart limb from limb. Now we have to play the whole next game as a spinoff Karen? Nahh... I'm gonna head out.

Pretty good! Ultimately my biggest criticism is that your companion wont shut the hell up and stop giving be constant advice on relatively simple puzzles. Like at least give me two minutes before you assume I'm stumped. ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥!

Otherwise, a direct improvement on GoW2018 in nearly every aspect.
Absolute trash button reading overpowered spongy enemies totally ruined any real proper challenge.

你份工同埋做你？
巴里，出嚟。你老豆同你講緊嘢。
馬丁，你會唔會同佢傾偈？
巴里，我同你講緊嘢呀！
你嚟緊？
攞晒所有嘢？
全部準備好！
做啦。我會追返。
唔好太長。
睇呢個！
瓦妮莎！
我哋仲喺度。
我叫你唔好嗌佢。
佢對嗌嘢冇反應！
噉點解要嗌我？
因為你唔聽！
我唔係聽緊呢個。
對唔住，我要走喇。
你去緊邊？
我見緊個朋友。
女仔？呢個係咪你決定唔到嘅原因？
拜拜。
我只係希望佢係蜜蜂式嘅。
佢哋每年喺帕薩迪納都有一個巨大嘅花遊行？
參加玫瑰錦標賽，係每個花店嘅夢想！
喺浮標上面，周圍都係花，人群歡呼。
一個錦標賽。玫瑰係咪參加運動會？
唔係。好，我有一個。
點解你唔係去到邊都飛？
好攰。點解你唔去到處跑？佢快啲。
係呀，好啦，我明喇，我明喇。
好啦，輪到你啦。
蒂沃。你可以淨係凍結直播電視？好癲呀！
你冇嗰個？
我哋有 Hivo ，但係佢係一種病。呢個係一種好可怕好可怕嘅疾病。
哦，我嘅。
傻蜜蜂！
你一定想刺晒嗰啲淫賤嘅人。
我哋盡量唔會刺痛。對我哋嚟講通常都係致命嘅。
所以你要留意你嘅脾氣。
好小心。
你踢牆，行下，寫封嬲信，然後丟咗出去。就好似任何情緒噉去處理佢：憤怒、妒忌、慾望。
